data.title: Airbus to deliver more planes this year, Spirit Aero hurdles delay A350 freighter
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) is expecting to deliver more planes in 2025 compared to a year ago, even as it faces continued supply chain challenges and broader uncertainty in light of global trade tensions.

The European aircraft maker expects to deliver around 820 commercial planes this year, slightly lower than the consensus estimate of 824 jets, but higher than 766 deliveries in 2024.

The outlook assumes no additional disruptions to global trade, air traffic, the supply chain, and Airbus' (OTCPK:EADSF) internal operations. It also excludes the impact of potential new tariffs on the company's business.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) pushed the entry-into-service date for the A350 freighter variant to the second half of 2027, from its earlier target of 2026. "Specific supply chain challenges, notably with Spirit AeroSystems (SPR), are currently putting pressure on the ramp up of the A350 and the A220," it said.

The company continues to target a monthly production rate of 12 A350s in 2028 and 14 A220s in 2026. The A320 family continues to ramp up towards a rate of 75 aircraft per month in 2027, while monthly A330 production is stabilizing at around rate 4.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) also expects the impact of the integration of certain Spirit (SPR) work on net cash to be broadly neutral, as compensation due from the supplier will offset the mid-triple-digit negative impact of the integration on free cash flow.

The plane maker is taking over some parts of Spirit (SPR) business, while rival Boeing (BA) is acquiring the majority of the struggling supplier.

Meanwhile, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) booked charges of €1.3B in its space operations in 2024, including €300M in Q4 resulting from the completion of an in-depth technical review.

data.title: European markets mixed as investors focus on earnings, tariffs; gold hits fresh record high
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.36%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.26%

France (CAC:IND) +0.39%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ticked up +0.07% on Thursday, after logging its worst day so far in 2025 in the previous session, amid the latest flurry of earnings releases.

Gains in the industrial goods and services sector were driven by a jump in Schneider Electric (OTCPK:SBGSF) shares after the electrical equipment maker reported record annual sales and profits.

Meanwhile, Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF) reported a record operating profit and revenue beat, while Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) reported an 8% year-on-year drop in adjusted operating profit, despite a 6% jump in revenue.

Elsewhere, traders continue to monitor developments in global trade and the possibility that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer, after U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposed tariffs on autos, chips, and pharmaceutical imports.

On the commodities front, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) surged above $2,950 per ounce on Thursday, reaching a record high amid sustained safe-haven demand.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.53%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.56%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.61%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: BE Semiconductor reports Q4 results
data.description:
• BE Semiconductor press release (OTC:BESIY): Q4 GAAP EPS of €0.74.
  • Revenue of €153.4M (-3.9% Y/Y)
  • Orders of € 121.9 million down 19.7% vs. Q3-24 and 26.7% vs. Q4-23 due primarily to decreased bookings for high performance computing and mainstream assembly applications
  • Gross margin of 64.0% decreased by 0.7 points vs. Q3-24 and 1.1 points vs. Q4-23 primarily due to adverse net forex influences
  • Net income of € 59.3 million increased 26.7% vs. Q3-24 and 8.0% vs. Q4-23 due to € 18.2 million of net tax benefits realized. As a result, net margin rose to 38.6% vs. 29.9% in Q3-24 and 34.4% in Q4-23
  • Cash and deposits of € 672.3 million at year-end increased 62.6% versus year-end 2023. Net cash of € 143.8 million increased € 33.1 million (29.9%) vs. Q3-24 and € 30.8 million (27.3%) vs. Q4-23

  • Q1-25 Outlook

    • Revenue expected to decrease 0-10% vs. the € 153.4 million reported in Q4-24
    • Gross margin expected to range between 63-65% vs. the 64.0% realized in Q4-24
    • Operating expenses expected to grow 10-20% vs. the € 47.6 million reported in Q4-24

data.title: Pantheon Resources hires ex-BP executive Max Easley as CEO
data.description:
Pantheon Resources (OTCQX:PTHRF) Thursday named Max Easley as Chief Executive Officer, succeeding Jay Cheatham.

Easley will be appointed as a member of the Pantheon (OTCQX:PTHRF) Board of Directors effective 28 February 2025, while Jay will continue to serve the company as a non-executive director for a period of handover to Max.

The appointment of Easley is another key step in the development of the Board and governance in preparation for a possible U.S. listing, the oil and gas company said.

Over the course of his career, Max has held executive roles at BP (BP), Apache (APA) and PETRONAS Canada.

data.title: NetEase reports Q4 results
data.description:
• NetEase press release (NASDAQ:NTES): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42.
  • Revenue of $3.7B.
  • Gross profit was RMB16.3 billion (US$2.2 billion), a decrease of 3.3% compared with the same quarter of 2023.
  • Total operating expenses were RMB8.5 billion (US$1.2 billion), a decrease of 15.1% compared with the same quarter of 2023.
  • Net income attributable to the Company's shareholders was RMB8.8 billion (US$1.2 billion). Non-GAAP net income attributable to the Company's shareholders was RMB9.7 billion (US$1.3 billion).[1]
  • Basic net income per share was US$0.38
